finding: Protected override audit files can be selected into a governed commit bundle as task-owned artifacts, but the active task direction lock may not automatically include them.
expectedBehavior: Any generated task-owned protected override audit included by the commit bundle should either be automatically allowed by the direction-lock gate or trigger a precise scope-amendment flow before staging.
evidenceOrRepro: FABLE-005 commit initially failed on `.atm/history/protected-override-audit/2026-07-12T15-59-52-259Z-POA-0e9840962464.json`; 0188 failed on two protected override audit files. Both were current-task artifacts but outside `taskDirectionLock.allowedFiles`; `tasks scope add` was required before commit.
followUp: Add protected-override audit ownership to the direction-lock allowed-file model and cover it in pre-commit + commit-wrapper tests.
